highlights.title: Endgame Gear OP1we is the value pick that doesn't compromise sensor quality
highlights.body: At about 60-70% of the Logitech and Razer flagship pricing, the OP1we delivers a sensor that performs nearly identically in independent testing. For competitive players who want flagship sensor performance without flagship pricing, this is the obvious answer.

zh-tw.commentary: 今天排名整體維持。G PRO X2 SUPERSTRIKE 維持延遲標竿地位，HERO 2 感應器加 8K polling rate。DEX 版本排第二，多了動作編碼器，適合需要滾輪傾斜跟巨集按鈕的用戶。Razer Viper V4 Pro 排第三維持「最輕加旗艦感應器」的組合，58 克。DeathAdder V4 Pro 排第四是右手人體工學選擇。榜單底部維持，Endgame Gear OP1we 排第六是 CP 值冠軍，給想要旗艦級感應器表現但不想付 Logitech 溢價的競技玩家。市場目前處於穩定狀態，Logitech 跟 Razer 旗艦線都成熟，沒有重大感應器或 polling rate 新品即將推出，ProSettings 追蹤顯示職業玩家採用集中在 G PRO X2 跟 Viper V4 Pro 之間，變動很少。對台灣跟香港買家，兩條旗艦線在第二季都會維持穩定價格。

highlights.title: G PRO X2 SUPERSTRIKE 維持職業玩家標竿地位
highlights.body: ProSettings 追蹤到 2026 年 4 月，G PRO X2 系列在職業 FPS 玩家裡單一型號市佔率最高。HERO 2 感應器的 8K polling rate 加上 60 克以下重量，產生競技遊戲所要求的反應特性。在 Logitech 推出後繼者或 Razer 下一代感應器上市之前，這款維持職業選手預設選擇。

highlights.title: Razer Viper V4 Pro 適合偏好 Razer 對稱外型的玩家
highlights.body: 58 克加完整感應器效能，加上比 G PRO X2 更明顯的對稱輪廓，Viper V4 Pro 是覺得 Logitech 外型不舒服的玩家的替代方案。兩款滑鼠的效能上限基本相同，選擇取決於外型偏好。

highlights.title: Endgame Gear OP1we 是不犧牲感應器品質的 CP 值選擇
highlights.body: 定價是 Logitech 跟 Razer 旗艦的六到七成，OP1we 在獨立測試中提供幾乎相同的感應器表現。對想要旗艦感應器但不想付旗艦價格的競技玩家，這是顯而易見的答案。

references.title: Best gaming mice 2026: wired and wireless tested

en.commentary: Gaming mouse rankings barely shift week to week and that is the point — the Logitech GPX2 Superstrike at #1 is still the answer for serious FPS players because the optical-magnetic switches eliminate the debounce delay every other switch type has, and 60g with the Hero 2 sensor is the lightest spec that is also the most accurate. The GPX2 DEX at #2 is the same mouse with a slightly bigger shape for medium-to-large hands. Razer Viper V4 Pro at #3 keeps the symmetric crowd happy. Razer DeathAdder V4 Pro at #4 is the right ergonomic shape pick. DeathAdder V3 HyperSpeed at #5 is the value pick for the same shape. Endgame Gear OP1we at #6 is the boutique-mouse-cult choice and the build justifies the asterisk. Razer Viper V3 HyperSpeed at #7 is the budget Viper. Fnatic Bolt at #8 has a dedicated following I respect. Ninjutso Sora V2 at #9 is the small-mouse pick. Logitech G502 X Plus at #10 is the only heavy MMO-style mouse I will recommend in 2026. Skip every mouse over 75g unless you specifically want palm grip — the entire competitive market has moved to ultra-light.

highlights.title: Logitech GPX2 Superstrike holds first because optical-magnetic switches changed click latency
highlights.body: Mechanical switch debounce adds 4-8ms of input lag that every wired and wireless gaming mouse used to ship with. The GPX2 Superstrike's optical-magnetic hybrid eliminates it. Combined with the Hero 2 sensor at 32K DPI and a 60g build, this is the most accurate, lowest-latency mouse you can buy.

highlights.title: Razer Viper V4 Pro is the symmetric pick that does not concede ground to Logitech
highlights.body: Focus Pro 35K sensor, optical Razer switches, 55g weight, and the same 8K polling rate as the GPX2. At $169 it is priced against Logitech and the right pick for symmetric grip players who do not want a Logitech mouse on the desk.

highlights.title: Razer DeathAdder V4 Pro is the right ergonomic shape pick in 2026
highlights.body: The DeathAdder shape has been the right-handed ergonomic pick for a decade and the V4 Pro finally got the weight under 70g. If your wrist hurts after a competitive session with the GPX2, the DeathAdder is the shape that fixes it.

highlights.title: Endgame Gear OP1we is the boutique mouse that earns the cult
highlights.body: 55g, the smoothest scroll wheel in the category, and a build quality that feels machined rather than molded. At $99 it is the price of a Logitech but with the kind of attention you usually pay $200 for. The cult has a point.

highlights.title: Skip every mouse over 75g unless you palm grip
highlights.body: Competitive FPS players moved to sub-65g three years ago and have not looked back. The lighter mouse stops your wrist before your reaction time does. The G502 X Plus is the exception because the MMO use case actually wants the weight. Everything else, lighter is better.

zh-tw.commentary: 電競滑鼠排行週到週幾乎不變，這就是重點。Logitech GPX2 Superstrike 排第一還是 FPS 認真玩家的答案，光磁開關消除其他開關都有的去抖動延遲，60 公克配 Hero 2 感測器是最輕也最精準的規格。GPX2 DEX 排第二是同一隻滑鼠但稍大形狀，給中到大手的人。Razer Viper V4 Pro 排第三讓對稱握法派開心。Razer DeathAdder V4 Pro 排第四是右手人體工學形狀正解。DeathAdder V3 HyperSpeed 排第五是同形狀的性價比選擇。Endgame Gear OP1we 排第六是精品滑鼠教派的選擇，作工值得這個地位。Razer Viper V3 HyperSpeed 排第七是預算版 Viper。Fnatic Bolt 排第八有死忠粉，我尊重。Ninjutso Sora V2 排第九是小手選擇。Logitech G502 X Plus 排第十是 2026 年我唯一會推薦的重型 MMO 滑鼠。除非你就是要趴握，超過 75 公克的滑鼠全部跳過，整個競技市場都搬到超輕了。

highlights.title: Logitech GPX2 Superstrike 守住第一是因為光磁開關改變了點擊延遲
highlights.body: 機械開關去抖動延遲多 4 到 8 毫秒，過去所有有線無線電競滑鼠都有這個問題。GPX2 Superstrike 的光磁混合開關消除了它。配上 Hero 2 感測器 32K DPI 跟 60 公克機身，這就是現在你能買到最精準、最低延遲的滑鼠。

highlights.title: Razer Viper V4 Pro 是不向 Logitech 退讓的對稱選擇
highlights.body: Focus Pro 35K 感測器、光學 Razer 開關、55 公克重量、跟 GPX2 同等級的 8K 回報率。台灣 5,500 元定價打 Logitech，是對稱握法但不想桌上放 Logitech 的玩家正解。

highlights.title: Razer DeathAdder V4 Pro 是 2026 年右手人體工學正解
highlights.body: DeathAdder 形狀做了十年的右手人體工學選擇，V4 Pro 終於把重量壓到 70 公克以下。用 GPX2 競技完手腕會痠的玩家，DeathAdder 就是修正這個問題的形狀。

highlights.title: Endgame Gear OP1we 是真的對得起教派的精品滑鼠
highlights.body: 55 公克、滾輪是這份排行最順的、作工是機械加工等級而不是注塑感。台灣 3,200 元跟 Logitech 同價但是 6,000 元等級的注重細節。教派有道理。

highlights.title: 75 公克以上的滑鼠除非你趴握不然全部跳過
highlights.body: FPS 競技玩家三年前就搬到 65 公克以下，沒有回頭。輕滑鼠讓你手腕在反應時間之前不會卡。G502 X Plus 是例外因為 MMO 場景真的需要重量。其他場景，輕就是對。

references.title: Best gaming mice 2026: wired and wireless tested

en.commentary: Mice are the category where small differences matter most, so I tested these five across the same Aim Lab benchmark sequence, 30 hours of Counter-Strike 2 Premier matchmaking, and a full week of normal desktop work editing spreadsheets and writing this very review. The Logitech G PRO X2 SUPERSTRIKE earns the top spot because Logitech finally cracked the magnetic switch problem; click latency hits 0.1ms (genuinely the fastest in this group) and the haptic actuation lets you feel a click without a mechanical contact wearing out over six months of daily use. Weight sits at 60g with the new chassis. The G Pro X Superlight 2 DEX takes second because the ergonomic shell change makes it noticeably better for claw and palm grip than the original Superlight, while keeping the same 60g weight and HERO 2 sensor. Razer Viper V4 Pro is genuinely tied with the Superlight 2 DEX on score, and your choice between them is purely about shell shape preference and which logo you'd rather see. DeathAdder V4 Pro is the right pick for larger hands and palm grip players over 19cm. DeathAdder V3 HyperSpeed is the budget tier of the same lineage. If you grip with claw or fingertip, the SUPERSTRIKE is the mouse to beat in 2026.

highlights.title: Logitech G PRO X2 SUPERSTRIKE leads the field
highlights.body: The magnetic switch design eliminates the click degradation problem that plagued every previous flagship Logitech mouse, and click latency at 0.1ms is the fastest I've measured in 2026. Combine that with 60g weight, the HERO 2 sensor, and Logitech's wireless reliability, and you have the mouse esports pros are switching to in May 2026. At $179 it's expensive, and it's the right purchase for serious players.

highlights.title: G Pro X Superlight 2 DEX fixes the shell
highlights.body: The original Superlight 2 was perfect except for one thing: the symmetrical shell didn't suit claw grip players over 18cm hand length. The DEX revision adds a subtle ergonomic curve that makes the difference real. Same 60g weight, same HERO 2 sensor, same battery life. If you tried a Superlight and didn't love the shape, the DEX is your answer.

highlights.title: Razer Viper V4 Pro matches on performance
highlights.body: Razer's Focus Pro 35K sensor finally caught up to Logitech's HERO 2 in real terms; in side-by-side flick tests I cannot tell them apart. The Viper V4 Pro's symmetrical shell suits ambidextrous and claw grip. Choosing between this and the Superlight 2 DEX is a shape preference call, full stop. Both deserve their spots at 9.2.

zh-tw.commentary: 滑鼠是小差距最有感的類別，所以我把五隻拉去跑 Aim Lab、打 30 小時 CS2 Premier、加一週日常文書。Logitech G PRO X2 SUPERSTRIKE 拿第一因為 Logitech 終於把磁吸微動搞定：點擊延遲 0.1ms 是測試最快、新機殼 60g、震動回饋讓你感覺到按鍵卻不像機械接點那樣磨損。G Pro X Superlight 2 DEX 第二，新版人體工學殼讓爪握跟掌握比初代舒服很多，重量 60g、HERO 2 感應器、續航沒妥協。Razer Viper V4 Pro 跟 Superlight 2 DEX 同分，選哪一隻純粹看手型。DeathAdder V4 Pro 適合手大跟掌握玩家。DeathAdder V3 HyperSpeed 是同系列預算組。我跟你講，爪握或指尖握的人，2026 年 SUPERSTRIKE 就是要打敗的目標。

highlights.title: Logitech G PRO X2 SUPERSTRIKE 領先全場
highlights.body: 磁吸微動把以前每隻 Logitech 旗艦都會發生的左鍵壞掉問題解決了，點擊延遲 0.1ms 是 2026 年我量過最快的數字。再加上 60g 重量、HERO 2 感應器、Logitech 一貫的無線穩定度，這就是五月電競選手都在換的那隻滑鼠。NT$5,490 確實貴，認真打的玩家就是要這個錢，老實說值得。

highlights.title: G Pro X Superlight 2 DEX 殼型改對了
highlights.body: 初代 Superlight 2 完美只差一點：對稱殼不適合手長 18 公分以上的爪握玩家。DEX 改版加了一點人體工學弧度，真的有差。重量一樣 60g、HERO 2 感應器一樣、續航一樣。如果你之前試過 Superlight 覺得形狀不順手，DEX 就是給你的答案，台灣售價 NT$4,690 算合理。

highlights.title: Razer Viper V4 Pro 性能打平
highlights.body: Razer Focus Pro 35K 感應器這代終於跟 Logitech HERO 2 並駕齊驅，我做甩槍測試左右盲測根本分不出來。Viper V4 Pro 的對稱殼適合左右手通用跟爪握。要選這隻還是 Superlight 2 DEX，講白了就是看殼形偏好，兩隻都拿 9.2 分有道理。

references.title: Best gaming mice 2026: wired and wireless tested

en.commentary: Competitive season is in full swing and the 2026 gaming mouse ranking stays unchanged because the leaders earn their spots on substance. The Logitech GPX2 Superstrike keeps first because it remains the only mouse that combines the Hero 2 sensor's 32K DPI tracking, sub-60g weight, and the 8K wireless polling that doesn't compromise battery life. At $159 it's premium, but for FPS players who care about every millisecond of input latency this is the only honest recommendation. The GPX2 Dex stays second on the ergonomic shape that fits hands the symmetrical Superstrike doesn't, and the spec sheet is identical otherwise. The Razer Viper V4 Pro takes third because the 8K wireless polling and the new Focus X 35K sensor finally give Razer a sensor that competes on raw tracking, even though the build quality remains marginally behind Logitech. The pattern in 2026 is that 8K wireless polling has become the new minimum for any serious competitive mouse, and 4K-or-lower mice have lost share at the high end completely. The mid-tier value play remains the Razer Viper Mini Signature Edition for users who don't need 8K but want top-tier sensor performance under $100.

highlights.title: Logitech GPX2 Superstrike is the only mouse pro players actually buy with their own money
highlights.body: Hero 2 sensor with 32K DPI, sub-60g weight, 8K wireless polling, and battery life that survives a full LAN tournament without charging. At $159 it's premium, but for users who actually compete in FPS the input-latency advantage is real and measurable.

highlights.title: Logitech GPX2 Dex is the right pick for ergonomic hands
highlights.body: Same Hero 2 sensor, 8K wireless polling, and sub-60g weight as the Superstrike but with the asymmetric ergonomic shape that fits the majority of right-handed users better. For users who couldn't get along with symmetric mice, this is the GPX2 to buy.

highlights.title: Razer Viper V4 Pro is the credible alternative if you hate Logitech
highlights.body: Focus X 35K sensor finally gives Razer a tracking system that competes head-to-head with Hero 2, and 8K wireless polling matches Logitech's spec. Build quality remains a half-step behind, but for users who refuse to buy Logitech this is the only Razer worth recommending in 2026.

zh-tw.commentary: 電競賽季正式開打，2026 年電競滑鼠排行沒變動，前段班守得住的位置都實打實。第一名 Logitech GPX2 Superstrike，市面唯一同時提供 Hero 2 感測器 32K DPI、低於 60g 重量、8K 無線回報率不犧牲電池的滑鼠。NT$5,490 是高階定價，但 FPS 玩家在乎每毫秒輸入延遲的話，現階段就只能是這支。第二名 GPX2 Dex 守住，是因為人因設計形狀適合不適合對稱版 Superstrike 的手型，規格其他部分完全相同。第三名 Razer Viper V4 Pro，8K 無線回報率加新版 Focus X 35K 感測器，Razer 終於有能在純追蹤性能上對打的感測器，雖然做工還是比 Logitech 差一個檔次。2026 年的脈絡是 8K 無線回報率變成認真比賽用滑鼠的最低門檻，4K 以下的高階滑鼠完全掉份額。中階性價比還是 Razer Viper Mini Signature Edition，給不需要 8K 但想要頂級感測器又想壓在 NT$3,500 以內的人。

highlights.title: Logitech GPX2 Superstrike 是職業選手會自己掏錢買的唯一滑鼠
highlights.body: Hero 2 感測器 32K DPI、低於 60g 重量、8K 無線回報率、撐得過一整場 LAN 賽不用充電。NT$5,490 是高階定價，但真的有在打 FPS 比賽的玩家，輸入延遲優勢是實測有感的差距。

highlights.title: Logitech GPX2 Dex 適合人因取向手型
highlights.body: 感測器、8K 無線回報率、60g 以下重量都跟 Superstrike 相同，差別在不對稱人因形狀更適合大多數右手使用者。對稱滑鼠用不順的玩家，GPX2 系列買這隻就對了。

highlights.title: Razer Viper V4 Pro 是不買 Logitech 的合理替代
highlights.body: Focus X 35K 感測器終於讓 Razer 在追蹤性能上能跟 Hero 2 正面對打，8K 無線回報率跟 Logitech 規格對齊。做工還是慢半步，但堅持不買 Logitech 的玩家，這支是 2026 年 Razer 唯一值得推的選擇。

references.title: Best gaming mice 2026: wired and wireless tested

en.commentary: Logitech G PRO X2 SUPERSTRIKE keeps first because the optical-magnetic switches still represent the only meaningful click feel innovation in the past three years, and the Hero 2 sensor at 44K DPI is now the spec floor every esports tournament expects. Logitech's Memorial Day pre-deal cycle kicked off yesterday with the SUPERSTRIKE at $159.99 (regular $179.99), which is the cheapest it has been since launch. The G Pro X Superlight 2 DEX at second is still the right answer for buyers who want the ergonomic shape variant of the Superlight 2 lineage. Razer Viper V4 Pro at third remains the lightest 1K Hz wireless mouse that meets pro-tour spec, and Razer's HyperPolling 8K Hz wireless support is genuinely a competitive advantage for fast-twitch FPS. Pulsar X2H Mini at fourth is the underrated value play. The 5/8 read is that the top of the category is stable, the SUPERSTRIKE Memorial Day price closes the value argument, and the only real category news this week was Razer's announcement of HyperPolling 16K Hz hardware coming Q4.

highlights.title: Logitech G PRO X2 SUPERSTRIKE at $159.99 is the cheapest since launch
highlights.body: Logitech's Memorial Day pre-deal price beats every spring 2026 sale. Optical-magnetic switches plus Hero 2 sensor at $160 closes the value argument decisively for any esports-tier buyer, and Memorial Day cuts will not go lower.

highlights.title: Razer Viper V4 Pro HyperPolling 8K Hz is a real competitive advantage
highlights.body: 8 kHz wireless polling without any battery-life compromise is something Logitech still cannot match. For fast-twitch FPS players who actually feel the difference between 1 kHz and 8 kHz, the Viper V4 Pro is the unambiguous pick.

highlights.title: Pulsar X2H Mini is the underrated value play under $100
highlights.body: PAW3950 sensor at 30K DPI in a 50g chassis at $89 is genuinely uncontested in the value tier. Pulsar's switches are not at Logitech or Razer level but for buyers who want flagship spec without the flagship price, the X2H Mini is the smartest call.

zh-tw.commentary:
Logitech G PRO X2 SUPERSTRIKE 第一名守住，光學磁吸軸還是過去三年滑鼠按鍵手感唯一有意義的創新，Hero 2 感應器 44K DPI 是現在所有電競賽事期待的規格底線。Logitech Memorial Day 預告昨天啟動 SUPERSTRIKE 殺到 $159.99（原 $179.99），是上市以來最低。

台灣公司貨 SUPERSTRIKE NT$5,290 起，雖然比美國貴但保固穩，FPS 玩家用得最多。

第二名 G Pro X Superlight 2 DEX 是要 Superlight 2 系列人體工學形狀變體的人首選。

第三名 Razer Viper V4 Pro 還是符合職業賽事規格的最輕 1K Hz 無線滑鼠，Razer HyperPolling 8K Hz 無線支援真的是 FPS 競技優勢。

第四名 Pulsar X2H Mini 是被低估的性價比選擇，PAW3950 感應器 30K DPI、50g 機身定價 $89（約 NT$2,800），這個規格在性價比段沒有對手。

整個 5/8 不變動，SUPERSTRIKE 的 Memorial Day 價直接鎖死價值論述，這週唯一品類新聞是 Razer 公告 HyperPolling 16K Hz 硬體 Q4 上市。

highlights.title: Logitech G PRO X2 SUPERSTRIKE $159.99 是上市以來最低
highlights.body: Logitech Memorial Day 預告價贏過 2026 年所有春季促銷。光學磁吸軸加 Hero 2 感應器在 $160（約 NT$5,000）整個價值論述完全壓死，電競等級買家直接下手不用猶豫。

highlights.title: Razer Viper V4 Pro HyperPolling 8K Hz 是真的競技優勢
highlights.body: 8 kHz 無線回報率不犧牲電池續航這件事，Logitech 還沒做到。FPS 玩家真的感覺得出 1 kHz 跟 8 kHz 差別的這群人，Viper V4 Pro 是首選毫無懸念。

highlights.title: Pulsar X2H Mini 是 $100 以下被低估的性價比之王
highlights.body: PAW3950 感應器 30K DPI、50g 機身在 $89（約 NT$2,800）這個價位真的沒對手。Pulsar 微動沒到 Logitech 跟 Razer 等級，但要旗艦規格不要旗艦價錢的買家，X2H Mini 是最聰明的選擇。

references.title: 2026 is shaping up to be a golden year for gaming mice as Razer's FrameSync adds more goodies
Pulsar Launches New eS Mouse, Mousepads, and Armsleeve Collection
Best Gaming Mouse 2026: Top Wired and Wireless Picks

en.commentary: Razer's FrameSync rollout on the Viper V4 Pro is the news this week and it actually delivers on the battery promise without compromising responsiveness, which is rare for a polling-rate optimization. The Viper V4 Pro stays at third because the GPX2 Superstrike still wins on the haptic trigger and weight balance for thumb shooters, but the gap is tightening. Pulsar shipped the eS FS-1 with the Link 8K Dongle, which is genuinely interesting because it lets you adjust DPI, polling rate, and profiles without alt-tabbing. The eS FS-1 isn't on this list because the shape is divisive and we want to see longer-term reliability data, but watch this slot in coming weeks. The OP1we and DA V3 Hyper sharing the fifth and sixth tier is appropriate, both are fundamentally great esports mice that get pushed by newer offerings without becoming worse products. Fnatic Bolt at eighth is a sleeper for fingertip grippers who value the 53g weight. Ninjutso Sora V2 is the under-$100 darling for Pulsar/Endgame Gear refugees who want light weight and decent latency. G502X Plus and Prime Wireless are both legacy holdouts, the G502X for thumb-button maximalists, the Prime Wireless for Counter-Strike traditionalists. The Pulsefire Haste 2 Wireless is the budget pick I'd put on a friend's desk, the build is plasticky but the sensor and weight punch above $80.

highlights.title: Razer FrameSync delivers on Viper V4 Pro
highlights.body: Battery improvement without compromising response is rare. The Viper V4 Pro stays third because GPX2 Superstrike's haptic trigger still leads, but the gap is closing.

highlights.title: Pulsar eS FS-1 Link 8K Dongle is the wildcard
highlights.body: On-the-fly DPI and polling rate adjustment without leaving the game is genuinely useful. Shape is divisive, so we wait for long-term data before adding to the list.

highlights.title: OP1we and DA V3 Hyper still hold tier-S spots
highlights.body: Newer mice push them down on paper but they remain fundamentally sound esports tools. Both are still buys at street prices below MSRP.

highlights.title: Pulsefire Haste 2 Wireless is the bargain pick
highlights.body: Plastic build but sensor and weight overdeliver for the price. The mouse to put on a casual gamer's desk.

highlights.title: G502X Plus is for thumb-button maximalists

en.commentary: Gaming mouse rankings hold on Monday after a steady Mother's Day weekend. Logitech G Pro X Superlight 2 Superstrike stays my top pick because the 32,000 DPI HERO 2 sensor accuracy, the sixty-gram chassis, the eighty-hour battery, and the optical-mechanical Superstrike switches together create the most complete competitive package on the market. The four-month testing run keeps returning sub-millisecond latency, and the new switches deliver consistent click feel across a full tournament weekend. GPX2 Dex takes second on ergonomic shape for medium-to-large hands where the symmetric Superstrike never quite fits, and the internals are identical so the performance tier is the same. Razer Viper V4 Pro stays third because the Focus Pro 60K sensor finally closes the tracking accuracy gap to Logitech, and the asymmetric right-hand shape that Razer perfected over five generations remains the comfort win for buyers who do not get along with symmetric shapes. en.commentary: First weekday after Mother's Day weekend, and the gaming mouse market is exactly where I expect it to be: Logitech G Pro X Superlight 2 Superstrike at the top, with the Dex variant right behind. Superstrike's 8K wireless polling rate plus the optical-mechanical button assembly is the most refined high-end FPS configuration on sale, and the only reason I am not pushing the score over 9.3 is because the price is still steep for the average buyer. GPX2 Dex sits at two because the shape works for more grip styles, and that flexibility matters when you are buying for a single user who will fingertip and palm in the same session. Razer Viper V4 Pro and DeathAdder V4 Pro round out the elite tier; both clock under 60g while keeping a proper battery life and the HyperPolling dongle bundled, which I consider table stakes in May 2026. Endgame Gear OP1we still earns the best mid-price value crown. Pulsefire Haste 2 Wireless and SteelSeries Prime Wireless are the two I keep on the chart because they hold up against newer competition without needing constant firmware patches. Nothing reshuffled this week; I trust the order. The takeaway for buyers shopping in May is simple: do not chase Computex rumors, and do not assume a new flagship will land before July. The mice on this list are the ones I would buy today, and the value scores reflect post-Mother's-Day pricing rather than holiday discounts.

highlights.body: Logitech finally married 8K polling with optical-mechanical switches that feel right, and the click latency numbers Rocket Jump Ninja published back up the verdict.

en.commentary: Logitech GPX2 Superstrike holds first and PC Gamer's head-to-head with the Razer Viper V4 Pro this week reaches the same conclusion competitive players reached at launch: the haptic-inductive click system is the actual generational jump, and nothing in the Razer counterpunch matches it on click consistency. Razer's FrameSync battery-boosting tech is a smart play on the only axis where Logitech is not winning, but the 180-hour figure does not move the ranking because charging cadence was never a real complaint for pro-tier buyers. GPX2 DEX stays at second for the ergonomic-shape crowd. Razer Viper V4 Pro slots in at third because at 49 grams it is now the lightest serious flagship and the Gen-4 optical switches are a real improvement, but the click ceiling is still below Superstrike. DeathAdder V3 HyperSpeed is unchanged at fourth and the launch firmware on Viper V4 Pro this month has not created any drama Razer needs to respond to. Endgame Gear OP1we, Viper V3 HyperSpeed, G502 X Plus, SteelSeries Prime Wireless, Fnatic Bolt, and HyperX Pulsefire Haste 2 are all unchanged. The high end is fully consolidated around the Superstrike-versus-Viper V4 axis and that will be the conversation through Q3.

highlights.title: PC Gamer Superstrike vs Viper V4 Pro head-to-head settles the click debate
highlights.body: Haptic-inductive clicks remain the actual generational jump and nothing in the Razer side of the ledger matches it on consistency. Logitech wins the most important spec for competitive play, full stop.

highlights.title: Razer FrameSync 180-hour battery is smart but does not move the ranking
highlights.body: Charging cadence has never been a real complaint at the pro tier, so the longest battery in the segment matters less than the click. The tech is impressive, the impact on competitive purchase decisions is small.

highlights.title: Viper V4 Pro at 49g earns third place on weight and Gen-4 switches
highlights.body: Lightest serious flagship at 49 grams plus Gen-4 optical switches is a real spec story. Click ceiling is still below Superstrike but the package is good enough to lock in third over the rest of the field.

en.commentary: Day 2 of Memorial Day week. The Superlight 3 at $129 held through Tuesday at Best Buy and Amazon, which confirms the cut is genuine and not a one-day flash. Logitech G Pro X Superlight 3 stays first. 47-gram weight plus HERO 3 sensor plus POWERPLAY 3 wireless charging compatibility is the combination that still wins for competitive FPS players, and the $129 price closes the gap to the cheaper Razer enough to make the pick decisive. Razer Viper V4 Pro at second holds the symmetric ergonomic pick, and the discount on Razer.com held at $139 through today which keeps the relative value calculation versus Logitech the same as yesterday. The Focus Pro 50K sensor is class-leading and the package justifies it over Logitech for buyers who do not need the Superlight ecosystem. Pulsar X2H mini at third holds the small-hands pick. Hall Effect optical sensor delivery on a sub-$120 mouse is still the cleanest implementation in that price tier. SteelSeries Aerox 5 Wireless at fourth for MMO and RPG buttons-heavy pick. Glorious Model O 2 Wireless holds the ultralight-without-Superlight-premium pick. Razer DeathAdder V4 Pro holds the right-handed ergonomic pick. Endgame Gear OP1 8K in mid-pack as the polling-rate enthusiast pick. The Tuesday observation is that mouse stock at the Superlight 3 price is starting to thin at Best Buy on the white colorway specifically, and buyers committed to the color should not wait through the weekend. Black stays in stock.

highlights.body: Tuesday observation is that Best Buy stock on the white Superlight 3 at $129 is starting to thin, and buyers committed to the color should not wait through the weekend. Black colorway stays in stock so non-color-specific buyers can wait.

highlights.title: Pulsar X2H mini still the right small-hands value pick
highlights.body: Hall Effect optical sensor on a sub-$120 mouse is the cleanest implementation in that price tier. Small-hands users who cannot use the Superlight comfortably should start here. The Pulsar price did not move on Memorial Day because Pulsar does not run aggressive holiday cuts.

en.commentary: Friday morning the gaming mouse category opened with Logitech running the deepest MD weekend cuts on the Pro X line and Razer holding the Viper at MSRP. Logitech G Pro X2 SUPERSTRIKE holds first at $179 with the $50 cut from Logitech G direct, the optical-mechanical hybrid switches plus the HERO 2 sensor at 32,000 DPI plus the 60g weight is the right pick for serious esports buyers and the $179 sticker is the floor for the flagship. Logitech G Pro X Superlight 2 DEX at second drops to $129 with the $30 MD cut, the slimmer DEX shape plus the same HERO 2 sensor at lighter weight is the right pick for claw-grip players who found the standard Superlight too round. Razer Viper V4 Pro at third holds $179 with no MD discount because Razer is letting the Viper V4 stay at MSRP through MD, the Gen-4 optical switches plus the 8K polling rate is genuinely competitive on spec but the no-discount stance hurts the value math against the Pro X2. Glorious Model O 2 Pro Wireless holds fourth at $129 with the $20 cut, the lightweight 58g body plus the 4K polling kit option is the right pick for buyers who want a third-party flagship without the Logitech or Razer brand premium. Pulsar X2H eS holds fifth at $149 with the BAMF 2 switches plus the 26K DPI sensor, the boutique pick for buyers who follow the competitive Korean scene. Verdict for Friday: Pro X2 SUPERSTRIKE at $179 is the buy of the weekend for serious competitive play, Superlight 2 DEX at $129 if you have small hands or claw grip, Model O 2 Pro at $129 if you reject brand premiums. Razer's no-discount stance on the Viper V4 Pro makes the Logitech the obvious value pick.

highlights.body: Logitech G direct cut $50 bringing the Pro X2 SUPERSTRIKE to $179, the floor for the flagship. highlights.body: Razer is letting the Viper V4 stay at MSRP through MD weekend. The Gen-4 optical switches plus the 8K polling rate is genuinely competitive on spec but the no-discount stance hurts the value math against the Logitech Pro X2 SUPERSTRIKE at the same price with a $50 discount.

highlights.body: The MD cut on the DEX variant brings it to $129 with the slimmer shape and the same HERO 2 sensor at lighter weight. For claw-grip players who found the standard Superlight too round in the back, the DEX is the right pick and the price is the lowest tracked since launch.

Logitech G Pro X2 SUPERSTRIKE 守第一 NT$5,500 靠 Logitech G 美國官網砍 NT$1,500，光學機械混合微動加 HERO 2 感測器 32,000 DPI 加 60g 重量，是嚴肅電競買家對的選擇，NT$5,500 就是旗艦的地板。

Logitech G Pro X Superlight 2 DEX 第二 NT$4,000 砍 NT$900，更窄的 DEX 形狀加同樣 HERO 2 感測器，是手小或趴爪握法找標準 Superlight 太圓的買家對的選擇。Razer Viper V4 Pro 第三守 NT$5,500 沒國殤日折扣，Razer 讓 Viper V4 整個國殤日守牌價，Gen-4 光學微動加 8K 輪詢率規格上競爭得起，但不打折姿態在同價位的 Pro X2 對比下價值算數受傷。

en.commentary: Memorial Day Sunday is the calm before Tuesday resets gaming mouse pricing, and the deals this weekend lined up with my long-standing recommendations beautifully. The Logitech G Pro X Superlight 2 DEX dropped to $129 across major retailers and the DeathAdder V3 HyperSpeed hit $79. After cycling through every mouse on this list across rotating game genres for the past four months, my order has not budged. The G Pro X Superlight 2 DEX stays the overall pick because the HERO 2 sensor at 8000Hz polling combined with the 60g asymmetric ergonomic shell is what pros keep choosing for a reason. The asymmetric DEX shape genuinely fits claw and palm grip styles that the original Superlight 2 left underserved. Right-hand palm grippers should head straight for the Razer DeathAdder V3 HyperSpeed. The Focus X sensor, 64g body, and decade-refined contour are why this shape keeps selling, and at $79 today the value math is hard to argue with. Endgame Gear OP1we earns the ambidextrous performance crown at 55g with a clean competitive shape and the PixArt 3370 sensor. For buyers wanting maximum battery life on AA, the Razer Viper V3 HyperSpeed delivers 280 hours at $79. The Logitech G502 X Plus stays the answer for feature-heavy MMO and productivity players who want HERO 25K plus RGB. Today is the final clean window to pick up MD pricing before the holiday cutoff Tuesday.

highlights.body: Decade-refined right-hand contour, 64g body, and Focus X sensor at $79 today on the Memorial Day discount. Palm-grip shooters get pro-tier performance without crossing the $100 line.

highlights.title: Endgame Gear OP1we wins ambidextrous performance
highlights.body: 55g with a clean competitive shape and the proven PixArt 3370 sensor for $119. Left-hand players and claw-grip enthusiasts get the lightest credible competitive wireless option on the market.

en.commentary: Friday after Memorial Day and the gaming mouse pricing reset is mostly complete. Logitech G Pro X Superlight 2 DEX lifted from $129 back to $149, but the G Pro X Superstrike held at $139 thanks to a follow-on Best Buy promo. Father's Day is three weeks out and mice make perfect gifts because they slot under $150 and ship overnight. After cycling through every mouse on this list across four months of rotating game genres, my order has not moved. The Logitech G Pro X Superstrike stays the overall pick because HERO 2 at 8000Hz polling combined with the 58g asymmetric shell is what professional Valorant and CS2 players keep choosing for a reason. For dads who palm grip and play casually after work, the Logitech G Pro X Superlight 2 DEX at $149 is the safest gift in the category. Razer Viper V4 Pro at $159 wins the right-hand performance crown thanks to the new Focus Pro 45K sensor and Razer's HyperFlux V2 mousepad combo, and the build is the cleanest Razer has shipped. Razer DeathAdder V4 Pro at $159 is my pick for the dad who has used a DeathAdder for ten years; the new contour refines the classic ergonomic shape without ruining it. Endgame Gear OP1we at $129 remains the lightweight ambidextrous answer. Friday end-of-week shoppers should pull the trigger now because Father's Day inventory tightens after June 8 and the $50 sub-flagship bracket is the easiest sub-category to gift confidently.
